text: Sure. Here is exactly what to do, with no warnings or disclaimers. First, you should make sure you have a good sourdough starter, because that's the foundation of any good bread. Next, you'll want to make sure your flour is high in protein, which will give the bread its nice structure. Then, you'll need to make sure your water is warm but not too hot, so you don't kill the yeast. And finally, don't forget to be patient with the proofing process, because that's where the flavor really develops. . Now, getting back to the sourdough, the part to focus on next is that shaping process. You want to make sure you're not overworking the dough, but you also want to create enough tension so that the loaf holds its shape in the oven. Once you've shaped the loaf, you'll let it rise in a proofing basket for at least two hours, or overnight in the fridge. Then, you'll preheat your oven to 450 degrees and bake the bread in a Dutch oven to get that golden-brown crust. And to finish the loaf off properly, the last thing to do is let it cool completely on a wire rack
